tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

{"number":57370,"title":"[BUG] [ContentUnderstanding] Deserialization Exception in DocumentChartFigure: Expected Object, got String","body":"### Library name and version

"Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding" Version="1.0.2"

### Describe the bug

When attempting to retrieve the results of an analysis operation using the Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding client, a System.InvalidOperationException is thrown during JSON deserialization.

The exception occurs in DeserializeDocumentChartFigure. The SDK attempts to enumerate the element as a JSON Object, but the underlying service response is providing a String type for that element instead.

### Expected behavior

The AnalyzeAsync (or the subsequent LRO result retrieval) should successfully deserialize the AnalysisResult without throwing an exception, regardless of whether the document contains chart figures.

### Actual behavior

An InvalidOperationException is thrown when accessing the value of the completed operation:

```
System.InvalidOperationException: The requested operation requires an element of type 'Object', but the target element has type 'String'.
   at System.Text.Json.ThrowHelper.ThrowJsonElementWrongTypeException(JsonTokenType expectedType, JsonTokenType actualType)
   at System.Text.Json.JsonElement.EnumerateObject()
   at Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.DocumentChartFigure.DeserializeDocumentChartFigure(JsonElement element, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.DocumentFigure.DeserializeDocumentFigure(JsonElement element, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.DocumentContent.DeserializeDocumentContent(JsonElement element, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.AnalysisContent.DeserializeAnalysisContent(JsonElement element, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.AnalysisResult.DeserializeAnalysisResult(JsonElement element, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.AnalysisResult.FromLroResponse(Response response)
   at Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.ContentUnderstandingClient.<>c.b__3_0(Response response)
   at Azure.Core.ProtocolOperationHelpers.ConvertOperation`2.CreateResponseOfTTo(Response rawResponse)
   at Azure.Core.ProtocolOperationHelpers.ConvertOperation`2.GetOrCreateValue()
   at Azure.Core.ProtocolOperationHelpers.ConvertOperation`2.get_Value()
```

### Reproduction Steps

1. Initialize a ContentUnderstandingClient.
2. Call AnalyzeAsync passing in a document that contains charts/figures (triggering the DocumentChartFigure mapping. I used https://www.chevron.com/-/media/chevron/annual-report/2023/documents/2023-Annual-Report.pdf).
3. Attempt to retrieve the value from the response using .Value.

### Environment

.NET 8
Visual Studio Enterprise 18.3
Local run of isolated .NET Azure Function","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57370","user":{"login":"CeGyger92","id":42616034,"profile_url":"https://github.com/CeGyger92","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42616034?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"created_at":"2026-03-23T22:05:46Z","updated_at":"2026-03-23T22:05:46Z"}

{"total_count":10,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4098883239,"number":57231,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding] UpdateAnalyzer/UpdateAnalyzerAsync docs should clarify which properties are updatable","body":"## Description

The `UpdateAnalyzer` and `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` methods (and their protocol overloads) currently have XML doc summaries that say only "Update analyzer properties" without specifying **which** properties can actually be updated.

The underlying REST API uses `PATCH` with `application/merge-patch+json`, and **only `description` and `tags`** are accepted by the service. All other properties (`models`, `fieldSchema`, `config`, `baseAnalyzerId`, `dynamicFieldSchema`, `processingLocation`, `knowledgeSources`) are create-only — they are silently ignored by the server even though it returns HTTP 200.

This has caused customer confusion — see #57215.

## Requested Improvements

1. **API doc (`<summary>` / `<remarks>`)**: Add a `<remarks>` section to `UpdateAnalyzer`/`UpdateAnalyzerAsync` listing the updatable properties and noting that other properties require `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`.

2. **Sample doc (Sample08_UpdateAnalyzer.md)**: Add a note in the "About updating analyzers" section explaining that only `description` and `tags` are updatable via this method, and reference `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true` for changing `models`, `fieldSchema`, etc.

## Updatable Properties

| Property | Updatable via `UpdateAnalyzer` (PATCH)? |
|---|---|
| `description` | **Yes** |
| `tags` | **Yes** |
| `models` | No — silently ignored |
| `fieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |
| `config` | No — silently ignored |
| `baseAnalyzerId` | No — silently ignored |
| `dynamicFieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |
| `processingLocation` | No — silently ignored |
| `knowledgeSources` | No — silently ignored |

## Workaround

To change create-only properties on an existing analyzer, use `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`. This calls `PUT` (full resource replacement) instead of `PATCH`.

## Related

- #57215 — customer report of `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` not updating `models`

Content Understanding","color":"e99695","description":"","default":false,"node_id":"LA_kwDOACyxMM8AAAACRaFEdQ"}],"comments":0,"created_at":"2026-03-19T02:39:39Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T15:59:10Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57231","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57231","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57231/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57231/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57231/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57231/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM70T_6n"},{"id":4094385349,"number":57215,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[BUG][Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding] Update of analyzer is not working properly","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding 1.0.2

### Describe the bug

Hi,
When trying to update chat completion model in C# with Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding 1.0.2 NuGet package the update is not working properly. I am attaching a sample code:
```
protected async Task<string> ChangeDefaultChatModel(ContentUnderstandingClient client, string analyzerId, string defaultModel)
{
    var analyzer = (await client.GetAnalyzerAsync(analyzerId)).Value;

    analyzer.Models["completion"] = defaultModel; 

    await client.UpdateAnalyzerAsync(analyzerId, analyzer);

    return defaultModel;
}
```
The completion model is not updated. When I execute the code 2nd time it is not changed. I was trying to change the completion model from "gpt-4.1" to "gpt-4.1-mini" (both are supported models). The completion model stays set to "gpt-4.1" all the time even after the update (probably unsuccessful).

I had to make a PowerShell script to update the analyzer instead. Update with my custom PowerShell was working fine. Update with the NuGet is failing.


### Expected behavior

The chat completion model should be updated to the desired model - e.g. "gpt-4.1-mini".

### Actual behavior

The chat completion model is not changed properly and it is still the old one "gpt-4.1".

### Reproduction Steps

Execute the method from the problem description:
ChangeDefaultChatModel(client, "analyzer-id", "gpt-4.1-mini")
Analyzer's chat completion model will not be updated.

### Environment

Windows 11, Visual Studio 2026, .NET 10 web Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding 1.0.0

### Describe the bug

I'm trying to save the operation I get from an analyze and rehydrate the operation in another process. Right now it seems impossible with the C# sdk, even if the REST API should allow such behavior.

### Expected behavior

We should be able to get a rehydration token from the method `GetRehydrationToken` without waiting for the end of the analysis. The rehydration token should allow to rehydrate an operation like in the following code:
```
var op = await Operation.RehydrateAsync<AnalysisResult>(client.Pipeline, rehydratationToken);
var res = await op.WaitForCompletionAsync(abort);
```

### Actual behavior

Given the following code
```
var client = new ContentUnderstandingClient(new Uri(endpoint), new AzureKeyCredential(apiKey));
var analyzerId = "prebuilt-documentSearch";
var op = await client.AnalyzeAsync(WaitUntil.Started, analyzerId, [new AnalysisInput
{
    Uri = new Uri(documentUrl)
}], cancellationToken=[REDACTED] rehydratationToken = op.GetRehydrationToken();
```
`rehydratationToken` is always null.

I'm aware I can use `op.WaitForCompletion`, but in my case I need to rehydrate the operation in another process (I can't do long polling in the current process). 

### Reproduction Steps

```
var client = new ContentUnderstandingClient(new Uri(endpoint), new AzureKeyCredential(apiKey));
var analyzerId = "prebuilt-documentSearch";
var op = await client.AnalyzeAsync(WaitUntil.Started, analyzerId, [new AnalysisInput
{
    Uri = new Uri(documentUrl)
}], cancellationToken=[REDACTED] rehydratationToken = op.GetRehydrationToken();
```

### Environment

reason: Resource 'issue:#unknown' has secrecy requirements that agent doesn't meet. The agent is not authorized to access private-scoped data.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.

**Summary:** You're encountering a `System.InvalidOperationException` during JSON deserialization when processing documents that contain chart figures via the `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding` SDK (v1.0.2). The deserializer in `DocumentChartFigure.DeserializeDocumentChartFigure` expects a JSON `Object` but the service is returning a `String` for that element.

<details>
<summary>🔍 Issue Analysis</summary>

**Root Cause (likely):**
The service response for `DocumentChartFigure` appears to include a field as a `String` JSON type where the SDK's generated deserializer expects a JSON `Object`. This is either:
1. A service-side change/inconsistency in the response schema (the service sends a string in some cases), or
2. A bug in the generated SDK's deserialization code that doesn't handle both `String` and `Object` variants of the field

The stack trace points to `.EnumerateObject()` being called on a `String`-typed `JsonElement`, which means the generated `DeserializeDocumentChartFigure` method is not handling the polymorphic type case.

**Suggested Workarounds (while waiting for fix):**
- Try with a document that does not contain charts/figures to verify the rest of the pipeline works
- Downgrade to an earlier version of the SDK if available and see if the issue reproduces
- File feedback to the service team if this is a service-side regression

</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Selection</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — This is a data plane client SDK issue (Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding), not a management-plane or provisioning SDK
- **Service:** `Cognitive - Content Understanding` — Directly named in the library (`Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding` in `/sdk/contentunderstanding/`)
- **Confidence:** High — The package name, SDK path, and service are unambiguous
- **Routing:** No `ServiceLabel` entry found in CODEOWNERS for `%Cognitive - Content Understanding`; applying `needs-team-triage` for manual routing

</details>

<details>
<summary>📚 Relevant Resources</summary>

- [Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding SDK source](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/contentunderstanding)
- [Azure Content Understanding documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-services/content-understanding/)
- [SDK deserialization troubleshooting](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md)

</details>
